title: XServer for WordPress Review for Japanese Businesses
metaTitle: XServer for WordPress Review: Plans, Staging and Limits
description: Review XServer for WordPress using official information on plans, staging, subaccounts, SLA, domains, support, trials, and the lack of mailbox hosting.
lead: XServer for WordPress is a Japan-focused platform for organizations and agencies that want to manage multiple WordPress sites through a dedicated panel, staging environments, resource allocation, and role separation. It does not provide mailboxes, so business email requires another service.
author: Luqevora Editorial Team

sections.heading: Bottom line: designed for standardized WordPress operations
body: The platform centralizes WordPress installation, migration, copying, updates, staging, resource allocation, and user access in WP Panel. It is most relevant when several sites or client accounts must follow a repeatable operating process.
It does not create email accounts. WordPress can send application messages within published limits, but business mailboxes need XServer Business, another mail host, or a productivity suite.
bullets: Best fit: agencies and companies managing several WordPress sites
Best fit: teams that need staging and site-specific access
Compare alternatives when website and email hosting must share one contract

sections.heading: Plans and current pricing structure
body: The provider lists three, six, twelve, twenty-four, and thirty-six-month terms. The pricing page displayed a promotion through September 15, 2026 at the verification date. Basic and Standard are shared-server plans; Premium and Enterprise are virtual dedicated plans.
Campaign and renewal prices can change. Save the provider quote and verify setup fees, billing term, and renewal terms before purchase.

sections.heading: WP Panel, staging and subaccounts
body: WP Panel supports new installations, automated migration, site copying, and grouped WordPress, plugin, and theme updates. Each site can have one staging environment on a separate server account and URL.

Subaccounts can be restricted to specific sites, which helps separate internal administrators, operators, agencies, and customers. Contract billing and some DNS operations remain outside subaccount permissions.

sections.heading: SLA, domains and support boundaries
body: The SLA sets a 99.99% monthly availability target for web, FTP, and database servers across all plans. Refund percentages depend on measured unavailability, and degraded performance is not automatically treated as downtime.

Eligible contracts include domain benefits, but co.jp and other organizational Japanese domains are limited to Standard, Premium, and Enterprise. Setup assistance is included within its published scope, while expert WordPress investigation is quoted separately on Basic and Standard and has monthly free allowances on higher plans.

sections.heading: Trial restrictions and final decision
body: The fourteen-day trial limits the combined number of production and staging sites to four. Mail sending, domain benefits, expert support, and setup assistance are restricted during the trial. Trial site data can continue after payment.

The service is a strong shortlist for Japan-based WordPress operations. Use a general business host or separate email provider when mailboxes, non-WordPress applications, or a broader server stack are required.

faqs.question: Does XServer for WordPress provide email accounts?
faqs.answer: No. It can send messages from WordPress within published limits, but it does not provide business mailboxes.

faqs.question: Can every trial feature be used for fourteen days?
faqs.answer: No. The trial limits site count and restricts mail sending, contract benefits, expert support, and setup assistance.

faqs.question: Is co.jp free on every plan?
faqs.answer: No. Organizational Japanese domains such as co.jp are listed for Standard, Premium, and Enterprise, not Basic.
